title: How to Make a Shaken Aviation Cocktail with a Precise Jigger
keyword: how to make aviation cocktail shaker
meta_description: Learn how to make an Aviation cocktail shaker style with a precise jigger. Step-by-step guide, tips, and the perfect Cocktail Shaker Set for bar‑quality results
html:
Crafting a classic Aviation cocktail doesn’t require a professional bar, but you do need the right technique and tools. Knowing how to make aviation cocktail shaker style drinks means mastering the shake, measuring with a precise jigger, and straining for that crystal‑clear finish. Below is a practical, no‑fluff walkthrough that any home mixologist can follow, plus a recommendation for the Cocktail Shaker Set that makes the process leak‑proof and effortless.

Gather the Essentials: Tools and Ingredients

Before you even think about shaking, line up every component. Using a dedicated jigger eliminates guesswork and ensures the cocktail’s balance stays spot‑on.

  • 2 oz gin (London dry is traditional)
  • ½ oz maraschino liqueur
  • ¼ oz crème de violette
  • ¾ oz freshly squeezed lemon juice
  • Ice cubes (large, clean cubes work best)
  • Fine‑mesh strainer
  • Bar spoon (optional, for stirring the garnish)
  • Coupe or martini glass, chilled
  • Precise jigger – the ½‑oz and 1‑oz measures in the Cocktail Shaker Set
  • Leak‑proof shaker – also part of the Cocktail Shaker Set

Having the Cocktail Shaker Set on hand means you’ll never mis‑measure or spill a drop. The set’s stainless‑steel jiggers lock into the shaker, keeping the pour exact and the workspace tidy.

Step‑by‑Step: How to Make an Aviation Cocktail Shaker‑Style

1. Cold‑Start the Glass – Place your coupe in the freezer for at least 5 minutes or fill it with ice water while you prep the drink. A cold glass keeps the cocktail crisp.

2. Measure with Precision – Using the ½‑oz side of the jigger from the Cocktail Shaker Set, measure the maraschino liqueur and crème de violette. Switch to the 1‑oz side for the gin and lemon juice. Precise measurements are the secret to a balanced Aviation.

3. Combine Ingredients – Add the gin, maraschino, crème de violette, and lemon juice into the shaker. No need for a separate mixing glass; the leak‑proof shaker handles everything.

4. Ice It – Fill the shaker about two‑thirds full with large ice cubes. The amount of ice determines how quickly the drink chills and dilutes; too little leaves the cocktail warm, too much makes it watery.

5. Shake Vigorously – Secure the lid and shake hard for about 12–15 seconds. You’ll feel the shaker become firm and hear the ice rattling. This is where the “how to make aviation cocktail shaker” method truly shines – the vigorous shake emulsifies the citrus and integrates the violet hue evenly.

6. Strain and Serve – Discard the ice from the chilled glass. Using the fine‑mesh strainer that comes with the Cocktail Shaker Set, double‑strain the mixture into the coupe. Double‑straining catches any tiny ice shards and ensures a silky texture.

7. Garnish – A classic Aviation is finished with a single lemon twist or a fresh cherry. Express the lemon peel over the drink to release its oils, then rim the glass before dropping it in.

The result is a pale‑blue cocktail with a subtle floral aroma and a bright citrus bite—exactly what you expect from a well‑shaken Aviation.

Why a Precise Jigger Matters

Even seasoned bartenders swear by the jigger. A ½‑oz measurement for the liqueurs locks in the delicate flavor balance; a full ounce of gin provides the backbone without overwhelming the violet notes. Using the integrated jiggers of the Cocktail Shaker Set eliminates the need for separate measuring tools, reducing cleanup and the chance of cross‑contamination between spirits.

Precision also speeds up the process. When you’re entertaining, every second counts, and a single‑handed pour from the jigger straight into the shaker keeps the workflow smooth. The stainless‑steel construction resists staining, so the vibrant blue of the Aviation never leaves a permanent mark on your measuring tools.

Tips for Perfecting the Shake

• Ice Quality – Use clear, filtered ice. Cloudy ice contains trapped air and impurities that can mute the cocktail’s flavor.

• Shake Angle – Hold the shaker at a slight tilt and shake in a circular motion. This creates a larger surface area for the ice to melt, chilling the drink faster.

• Temperature Check – After shaking, the shaker should be cold to the touch. If it feels warm, add a few more ice cubes and shake a few seconds longer.

• Strain Twice – The fine‑mesh strainer catches any ice shards, but a second pass through a chilled Hawthorne strainer (also included in the Cocktail Shaker Set) guarantees a pristine finish.

Cleaning and Maintaining Your Cocktail Shaker Set

After you’ve enjoyed your Aviation, rinse the shaker, jiggers, and strainer with warm water. For stubborn residue, a drop of dish soap and a soft brush will do the trick. Dry each piece thoroughly to prevent water spots. Because the set is stainless steel, it’s dishwasher‑safe, but hand‑washing prolongs the polish and keeps the seals tight.

Store the shaker with the lid loosely placed on top to allow any moisture to evaporate. Keep the jiggers in a drawer or on a magnetic bar strip; the set’s sleek design looks good even when not in use.

FAQ

Can I substitute the gin in an Aviation?

Yes. While London dry gin offers the classic botanical profile, a more floral gin can accentuate the violet notes. Just keep the volume the same (2 oz) to maintain balance.

Do I really need a second strainer?

Double‑straining isn’t mandatory, but it removes tiny ice particles that can cloud the drink. The fine‑mesh strainer in the Cocktail Shaker Set does most of the work; the extra step is for perfectionists.

What’s the best way to chill the glass?

Place the coupe in the freezer for at least five minutes, or fill it with ice water while you shake. A cold glass keeps the Aviation from warming too quickly after pouring.
